title: Display status preserves per-fact freshness across adapter loss

preconditions.description: The harness holds the inherited device-keyed lease before any device or fixture access.
The harness recorded session generation, display, layout, lock state, ATX-observable state, browser identity, and fixture identity.
Snapshot and event caches contain deliberately unequal signal, resolution, and FPS observation times.
The harness provisions each linked tool call with its valid owned session, required capability, and a separately captured fresh observation when the handler consumes one.

fault_script.action: Lose or replace the shared DeviceRpcAdapter binding at the tool-specific read or mutation boundary.
fault_script.expected_effect: Reads return explicit errors or qualified cached freshness; mutations use legal pre/post-write outcomes and are never replayed.

steps.expect: Signal, resolution, and FPS return independent source, time, age, and freshness values.
Status returns explicitly stale or unknown cached facts with no unified health inference.
The jetkvm_display_capture call under mid-flight binding loss returns an explicit read freshness/error or legal pre/post-write mutation outcome with no replay.
The jetkvm_display_status call under mid-flight binding loss returns an explicit read freshness/error or legal pre/post-write mutation outcome with no replay.
The jetkvm_input_keyboard call under mid-flight binding loss returns an explicit read freshness/error or legal pre/post-write mutation outcome with no replay.
The jetkvm_input_mouse call under mid-flight binding loss returns an explicit read freshness/error or legal pre/post-write mutation outcome with no replay.
The jetkvm_input_paste call under mid-flight binding loss returns an explicit read freshness/error or legal pre/post-write mutation outcome with no replay.
The jetkvm_input_release call under mid-flight binding loss returns an explicit read freshness/error or legal pre/post-write mutation outcome with no replay.
The jetkvm_power_control call under mid-flight binding loss returns an explicit read freshness/error or legal pre/post-write mutation outcome with no replay.
The jetkvm_session_connect call under mid-flight binding loss returns an explicit read freshness/error or legal pre/post-write mutation outcome with no replay.
The jetkvm_session_reconnect call under mid-flight binding loss returns an explicit read freshness/error or legal pre/post-write mutation outcome with no replay.
The jetkvm_session_status call under mid-flight binding loss returns an explicit read freshness/error or legal pre/post-write mutation outcome with no replay.

pass.assertion: Signal, resolution, and FPS retain independent sources, times, ages, and freshness while proxy streaming is absent.
Mid-flight reads error or return explicitly stale cached qualification; ATX uses pre-write or post-write outcome classification without replay.
A cache read after channel loss is returned only with explicit stale freshness; an unobserved fact is none, null, and unknown.

evidence.source: The linked focused assertion and ordered fake, replay, or live event record.
The linked focused assertion and ordered fake, replay, or live event record.
The linked focused assertion and ordered fake, replay, or live event record.

restore.action: Invoke generation-correlated emergency input release when a session exists.
restore.assertion: No held key, button, wheel producer, macro, or paste producer remains.

restore.action: Stop and join any Reliable Paste producer.
restore.assertion: Reliable Paste is inactive and its correlated producer is joined.

restore.action: Verify the fixture reports zero held keys and buttons.
restore.assertion: Held keyboard and pointer state is empty.

restore.action: Close only the device session created by this story and its browser resources.
restore.assertion: No story-owned browser, channel, or device session remains.

restore.action: Clear deterministic faults and restore the recorded fixture baseline.
restore.assertion: The next story starts only after the safe baseline is verified.

restore.action: Restore the recorded ATX-observable baseline using the reviewed fixture procedure while retaining the device lease.
restore.assertion: The baseline ATX-observable state is proven before the next story starts.

privacy.rule: Persist only allowlisted opaque identifiers, sanitized versions, counts, durations, hashes, outcomes, verification, and restoration results.
